meta.topic: Companion memory system — qualitative observations (session sentiments, repo impressions, user patterns, notable moments) with rule-based and Haiku-generated sources, CLI inspection

lockedDecisions: Four observation categories: session sentiments, repo impressions, user patterns, notable moments
Two observation sources: rule-based detectors and Haiku-generated qualitative summaries
Haiku produces a SINGLE observation per session, tagged to the most relevant category
Retention policy: fixed count (e.g., last 200), FIFO eviction
Commentary selection: recency-based, scoped to the current repo
CLI is read-only — no editing or deleting individual observations
Memory feeds into commentary via <memory> prompt section
CLI inspection via sisyphus companion memory subcommand
openQuestions: Repo scoping for CLI — should sisyphus companion memory accept a --repo filter? (unanswered)
Parallel vs sequential execution of rule detectors and Haiku call at session end (unanswered)
Module interface style for companion-memory.ts — pure functions, singleton, or class (unanswered)
